evals.prompt: My Claude Code sessions feel really expensive lately, especially in this Bookie project. Can you audit what's eating up my context window? I have a bunch of skills, MCP servers, memory bank files... I want to know exactly where the tokens are going and what I can cut.
evals.expected_output: A structured token audit report covering all context sources (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, memory, skills, MCP), with a context breakdown table showing line/char/token counts per source, benchmark checks against thresholds, detected issues categorized by severity, and prioritized recommendations with estimated savings. Should also save a token-audit.json snapshot.
expectations: Report includes a Context Breakdown table with columns for Source, Lines, Chars, Tokens, Category, and Status
Measures global CLAUDE.md (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md)
Measures project CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md
Measures auto-memory MEMORY.md from ~/.claude/projects/ directory
Measures memory bank files (.memory-bank/*.md)
Measures skill descriptions from .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md frontmatter
Analyzes MCP servers and counts deferred tools with ~50 tokens per tool estimate
Benchmark checks include CLAUDE.md line count against 200-line threshold
Benchmark checks include MEMORY.md line count against 200-line threshold
Issues are categorized as Critical, Warning, or Info severity levels
Recommendations include estimated token savings
Report includes a Limitations section mentioning runtime costs are not measured
Saves or mentions saving token-audit.json snapshot

evals.prompt: I just set up a new project with 4 MCP servers and I'm worried about context overhead. Run a token optimization check — I specifically want to understand the MCP cost breakdown and whether my CLAUDE.md files are too long.
evals.expected_output: A token audit focusing on MCP analysis using the 3-tier model (DEFERRED/LOADED/RUNTIME) and CLAUDE.md size benchmarks. Should clearly distinguish between always-on deferred costs and on-demand loaded costs.
expectations: MCP analysis distinguishes between DEFERRED tier (~50 tok/tool, always present) and LOADED tier (~200-400 tok/tool, on-demand)
MCP analysis mentions RUNTIME tier as not measurable statically
Reports MCP deferred cost as baseline (not loaded schema costs)
Checks each CLAUDE.md file against the 200-line threshold
Identifies if any MCP servers have no corresponding allow rules (possibly unused)
Total always-on token count is reported

evals.prompt: I suspect there's a lot of duplicated content between my CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and memory bank files. Also I have like 12 skills and some of them might have really long descriptions eating my context. Can you do a full audit and tell me what to consolidate?
evals.expected_output: A token audit with emphasis on duplication detection across always-on files and skill description analysis. Should identify specific duplicated content and recommend canonical locations.
expectations: Scans for duplicated content across CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and memory bank files
Identifies specific duplicated information (not just generic warnings)
Counts total skill descriptions and their combined character/token cost
Checks combined skill description size against the 16,000 character threshold
Checks for over-specified CLAUDE.md (workflows that should be skills, file-specific rules that should be in .claude/rules/)
Recommendations specify which file should be the canonical location for duplicated content
